title: Dephosphorylation of 5´-ends of DNA using rSAP (M0371)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"d7b42","text":"Protocol for Dephosphorylation of 5´-ends of DNA using rSAP (M0371).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"bv5ga","text":"Dephosphorylation of 5´- ends of DNA in Restriction Enzyme Reaction","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":67}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4h7hc","text":"The phosphate can be added directly into the digestion reaction during or after DNA digestion","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eqlrm","text":"rSAP is active in all NEB restriction enzyme buffers","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5snbr","text":"The restriction enzyme should be heat inactivated at the same time as the phosphatase after digest and dephosphorylation","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7btd7","text":"If restriction enzyme(s) cannot be heat inactivated, DNA purification is required before ligation","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"d34s1","text":"Please refer to the Safety Data Sheets (SDS) for health and environmental hazards. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare a 20 µL reaction as follows: A B 1 DNA 1 pmol of DNA ends* 2 CutSmart® Buffer (10X) 2 µl 3 rSAP 1 unit 4 H2O, purified to 20 µl** DNA1 pmol of DNA ends*CutSmart® Buffer (10X)2 µlrSAP1 unitH2O, purifiedto 20 µl** * 1 pmol of DNA ends is about 1 µg of a 3 kb plasmid **Scale larger reaction volumes proportionally
Stop reaction by heat-inactivation at 65 °C for 00:05:00 .
creator: New England   Biolabs
